question: What is the primary advantage of each microservice owning its own database?
options: Each service can use a different database vendor without coordination
Services can be deployed, scaled, and have their schema evolved independently without affecting other services
It reduces total storage costs by avoiding redundant data
Spring Boot auto-configures separate connection pools for each schema

question: What does @FeignClient(name = "user-service") do when combined with Eureka service discovery?
options: It creates a singleton bean that pings user-service every 30 seconds
It generates a type-safe HTTP client that resolves 'user-service' to an actual host and port via Eureka at runtime
It hardcodes the URL as http://user-service and requires a matching /etc/hosts entry
It imports all REST endpoints from user-service and exposes them locally

question: Two microservices share the same database. Why is this considered an anti-pattern?
options: Spring Boot only supports one database connection per application
Shared database creates tight coupling — a schema change in one service can break the other, and they can't be deployed independently
Databases can only handle connections from a single application at a time
It violates the CAP theorem by making the system non-consistent

question: What does a Circuit Breaker do when a downstream service is consistently failing?
options: It retries the request indefinitely until the downstream service recovers
It opens the circuit — calls immediately return the fallback result instead of attempting the network call
It reroutes requests to a backup database automatically
It sends an alert email to the operations team and pauses all traffic

question: Why is an API Gateway valuable in a microservices architecture?
options: It combines all microservice databases into a single queryable schema
It provides a single external entry point for routing, authentication, rate limiting, and SSL termination — clients don't need to know about individual services
It replaces Eureka for service discovery by maintaining a central service registry
It runs all microservices as threads inside a single JVM to reduce deployment overhead
